title: Action Film Actors
description: You are given three tables: actors, films with genres, and a film_cast table. Find distinct names of actors who appeared in at least one 'Action' film, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
actors(id, name, nationality)
films(id, title, genre, year)
film_cast(id, film_id, actor_id, role)
outputFormat: One actor name per line, ordered alphabetically. No duplicates.
constraints: Use two JOINs and DISTINCT. Filter WHERE genre = 'Action'. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT a.name FROM actors a JOIN film_cast fc ON a.id = fc.actor_id JOIN films f ON fc.film_id = f.id WHERE f.genre = 'Action' ORDER BY a.name;
hint: Join actors → film_cast → films, filter by genre = 'Action', use DISTINCT.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE actors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, nationality TEXT); CREATE TABLE films (id INTEGER, title TEXT, genre TEXT, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE film_cast (id INTEGER, film_id INTEGER, actor_id INTEGER, role TEXT); INSERT INTO actors VALUES (1,'Alice','US'),(2,'Bob','UK'),(3,'Charlie','AU'); INSERT INTO films VALUES (1,'Speed Rush','Action',2020),(2,'The Play','Drama',2019),(3,'High Impact','Action',2021); INSERT INTO film_cast VALUES (1,1,1,'Lead'),(2,1,2,'Support'),(3,2,3,'Lead'),(4,3,1,'Lead');

CREATE TABLE actors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, nationality TEXT); CREATE TABLE films (id INTEGER, title TEXT, genre TEXT, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE film_cast (id INTEGER, film_id INTEGER, actor_id INTEGER, role TEXT); INSERT INTO actors VALUES (1,'Diana','CA'),(2,'Eve','FR'),(3,'Frank','DE'),(4,'Grace','JP'); INSERT INTO films VALUES (1,'Fast Pursuit','Action',2022),(2,'Romance','Drama',2021),(3,'Storm','Action',2023); INSERT INTO film_cast VALUES (1,1,1,'Lead'),(2,1,3,'Support'),(3,2,2,'Lead'),(4,3,4,'Lead'),(5,3,1,'Support');
